servers.name: Brave Search
servers.description: Web and local search using the Brave Search API. Returns organic results, news, and local listings.

env_required.description: Brave Search API key — get one free at https://api.search.brave.com

servers.name: Fetch
servers.description: Fetch any URL and return its content as markdown or raw HTML. Useful for reading web pages, APIs, and documentation.

env_optional.description: Custom User-Agent string for HTTP requests

servers.name: Filesystem
servers.description: Read, write, move, search and manage local files and directories. Sandboxed to allowed paths.

env_required.description: Absolute path of directory the server may access

servers.name: Git
servers.description: Read and manipulate local Git repositories — log, diff, blame, branches, commits, and status.

servers.name: GitHub
servers.description: Interact with GitHub repositories — search code, manage issues, pull requests, files, and branches via the GitHub API.

env_required.description: GitHub personal access token with repo scope

servers.name: GitLab
servers.description: Interact with GitLab projects — manage issues, merge requests, files, pipelines, and repositories.

servers.name: Sentry
servers.description: Query Sentry for errors, issues, events, releases, and project stats. Useful for debugging and monitoring.

env_required.description: Sentry auth token (Settings → API → Auth Tokens)

servers.name: Playwright
servers.description: Full browser automation via Microsoft Playwright — navigate, click, fill, screenshot, extract content, run JS.

servers.name: Puppeteer
servers.description: Browser automation — navigate pages, take screenshots, click elements, fill forms, evaluate JavaScript.

servers.name: Google Maps
servers.description: Geocoding, reverse geocoding, directions, place search, elevation, and distance matrix via the Google Maps API.

servers.name: Google Drive
servers.description: Search, read, list, and export files from Google Drive including Docs, Sheets, and Slides.

servers.name: Notion
servers.description: Read and write Notion pages, databases, blocks, and comments. Official Notion MCP integration.

servers.name: Slack
servers.description: Post messages, reply to threads, list channels, and retrieve Slack conversation history.

servers.name: Discord
servers.description: Send messages, manage channels, read message history, and interact with Discord servers via the bot API.

servers.name: Linear
servers.description: Manage Linear issues, projects, cycles, and teams. Create, update, and search issues programmatically.

servers.name: Jira & Confluence (Atlassian)
servers.description: Read and write Jira issues, projects, sprints, and Confluence pages via the Atlassian REST API.

servers.name: Stripe
servers.description: Interact with Stripe — query customers, payments, subscriptions, invoices, and products via the Stripe API.

servers.name: PostgreSQL
servers.description: Read-only and read-write access to a PostgreSQL database. Run queries, list schemas, describe tables.

servers.name: SQLite
servers.description: Create and query SQLite databases. Run SQL queries, inspect schemas, and build business intelligence tools.

env_required.description: Absolute path to the SQLite .db file

servers.name: MySQL
servers.description: Connect to MySQL/MariaDB databases, run queries, list tables, describe schemas, and execute DDL/DML.

servers.name: MongoDB
servers.description: Query and manage MongoDB collections — find, insert, update, delete, aggregate, and manage indexes.

servers.name: Redis
servers.description: Interact with Redis — get/set keys, manage hashes, lists, sets, streams, and pub/sub channels.

servers.name: Supabase
servers.description: Query Supabase tables, run SQL, manage auth users, storage buckets, edge functions, and realtime channels.

env_required.description: Service role key from Project Settings → API

servers.name: Cloudflare
servers.description: Manage Cloudflare Workers, KV, D1, R2, and DNS records. Deploy and query Workers from the command line.

servers.name: AWS Knowledge Base (Bedrock)
servers.description: Retrieve and query Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases using RAG for enterprise document search.

servers.name: HubSpot
servers.description: Manage HubSpot CRM — contacts, companies, deals, tickets, properties, and timeline events.

servers.name: Shopify
servers.description: Manage Shopify stores — products, orders, customers, inventory, and collections via the Admin API.

servers.name: Airtable
servers.description: Read, create, update, and delete Airtable records. List bases, tables, and fields. Run formula queries.

servers.name: Gmail
servers.description: Read, send, search, and manage Gmail messages, threads, labels, and attachments.

servers.name: Google Calendar
servers.description: Create, read, update, and delete Google Calendar events. List calendars and manage attendees.

env_required.description: OAuth2 refresh token with calendar scope

servers.name: Vercel
servers.description: Manage Vercel deployments, projects, domains, environment variables, and logs via the Vercel API.

servers.name: Docker
servers.description: Manage Docker containers, images, volumes, and networks. Run, stop, inspect, and exec into containers.

servers.name: Kubernetes
servers.description: Manage Kubernetes clusters — list and describe pods, deployments, services, logs, and apply manifests.

servers.name: AWS
servers.description: Interact with AWS services — S3, EC2, Lambda, CloudWatch, DynamoDB, SQS, and more via the AWS SDK.

servers.name: Figma
servers.description: Inspect Figma files, frames, components, and design tokens. Extract styles, assets, and layout properties.

servers.name: Twilio
servers.description: Send SMS, WhatsApp messages, make voice calls, and manage Twilio phone numbers and conversations.

servers.name: SendGrid
servers.description: Send transactional and marketing emails, manage contacts and lists, query stats via the SendGrid API.

servers.name: Asana
servers.description: Manage Asana tasks, projects, teams, and workspaces. Create, update, search, and complete tasks.

servers.name: Google Sheets
servers.description: Read and write Google Sheets — get values, update cells, append rows, create/format spreadsheets.

env_required.description: OAuth2 refresh token with sheets scope

servers.name: Pinecone
servers.description: Query and manage Pinecone vector indexes — upsert, query by vector, list namespaces, and delete vectors.

servers.name: Elasticsearch
servers.description: Index, search, and manage Elasticsearch documents. Full-text search, aggregations, and index management.

servers.name: OpenAI
servers.description: Access OpenAI models — completions, embeddings, image generation (DALL-E), and assistants API.

servers.name: YouTube
servers.description: Search YouTube videos, get transcripts/captions, retrieve channel info and video metadata.

env_optional.description: YouTube Data API v3 key (required for search/metadata)

servers.name: Spotify
servers.description: Control Spotify playback, search tracks and albums, manage playlists, and get recommendations.

env_required.description: OAuth redirect URI registered in your Spotify app

servers.name: X / Twitter
servers.description: Post tweets, read timelines, search tweets, manage DMs and lists via the X/Twitter API v2.

servers.name: Memory (Knowledge Graph)
servers.description: Persistent knowledge graph memory. Create entities, store relations, and query structured facts across sessions.

env_optional.description: Path to the knowledge graph JSON file (default: in-memory)

servers.name: Sequential Thinking
servers.description: Structured multi-step reasoning tool. Breaks complex problems into sequential thoughts with revision support.

servers.name: Time
servers.description: Get current time in any timezone, convert between timezones, and format timestamps.

servers.name: OpenWeather
servers.description: Current weather and 5-day forecasts for any city using the OpenWeatherMap API.

servers.name: Salesforce
servers.description: Query and manage Salesforce objects — leads, contacts, accounts, opportunities, cases via SOQL and REST API.

servers.name: EverArt
servers.description: Generate AI images using EverArt's image generation API. Create artwork, illustrations, and visual assets.

servers.name: Perplexity
servers.description: AI-powered search and summarisation using Perplexity's Sonar API. Returns cited, up-to-date answers.

servers.name: Datadog
servers.description: Query Datadog metrics, logs, monitors, dashboards, and incidents for observability and alerting.

servers.name: GitHub Actions
servers.description: Trigger, monitor, and inspect GitHub Actions workflow runs, jobs, and artifacts.

servers.name: Everything (Test Server)
servers.description: Reference MCP server for testing. Exposes all MCP primitive types: tools, resources, prompts, sampling.

servers.name: Browser Agent
servers.description: Playwright-powered browser automation agent. Navigates pages, fills forms, extracts content, and executes multi-step web workflows. Runs as a self-registering Claude agent (openclaw).

env_required.description: Anthropic API key — or leave blank if using the LLM proxy

servers.name: Browser Agent (Docker)
servers.description: Containerised Playwright browser agent. Identical capabilities to the subprocess variant but runs in an isolated Docker container — ideal for server deployments and CI pipelines.

env_required.description: Anthropic API key passed into the container

env_optional.description: Override ORCHESTRATOR_URL inside the container. Required on macOS/Windows where --network=host is not available. Example: http://192.168.1.10:8000

os_support.macos: ✅ Docker Desktop ≥4.x required. Set ORCHESTRATOR_EXTERNAL_URL to your LAN IP or host.docker.internal
os_support.windows: ✅ Docker Desktop + WSL2 required. Set ORCHESTRATOR_EXTERNAL_URL to your LAN IP or host.docker.internal

servers.name: Code Execution Agent
servers.description: Sandboxed multi-language code execution agent (Python, Node.js, Ruby, Go, Bash). Runs as a self-registering Claude agent (openclaw). Includes live kill-switch and 9-layer security model.

os_support.linux: ✅ Full support including POSIX resource limits
os_support.macos: ✅ Full support including POSIX resource limits

servers.name: Code Execution Agent (Docker)
servers.description: Containerised sandboxed code execution. Same capabilities as the subprocess variant but fully isolated in Docker — recommended for production to prevent host filesystem access.

env_optional.description: Override orchestrator URL inside the container (required on macOS/Windows)

servers.name: Filesystem Agent (Docker)
servers.description: Containerised file and directory operations agent. Bind-mount the directories you want to expose and they appear inside the container. Ideal for isolating file access to specific paths.

env_optional.description: Override orchestrator URL inside the container
Colon-separated list of allowed paths inside the container (default /workspace)

servers.name: Tavily Search
servers.description: AI-native web search API designed for LLM agents. Returns clean, relevant search results with optional content extraction — no scraping needed.

env_required.description: Tavily API key — get one free at https://tavily.com

servers.name: Exa Search
servers.description: Semantic and keyword web search with full content retrieval. Returns high-quality results from a neural search engine — ideal for research and RAG pipelines.

env_required.description: Exa API key — get one at https://exa.ai

servers.name: Neon (Serverless Postgres)
servers.description: Manage Neon serverless PostgreSQL projects, branches, and databases. Run SQL queries, manage connection strings, and create/delete branches for dev workflows.

env_required.description: Neon API key — get one at https://neon.tech/docs/manage/api-keys

servers.name: Qdrant
servers.description: Vector database for AI applications. Store, search, and manage high-dimensional vectors with payloads. Supports similarity search, filtering, and collections management.

env_required.description: Qdrant instance URL e.g. http://localhost:6333 or your Qdrant Cloud URL
Qdrant API key (required for Qdrant Cloud, optional for local)

servers.name: Weaviate
servers.description: AI-native vector database. Semantic search, hybrid search, and object storage with multi-modal support. Integrates with OpenAI, Cohere, and Hugging Face embeddings.

env_required.description: Weaviate API key (required for Weaviate Cloud)

servers.name: Upstash (Redis + Vector + QStash)
servers.description: Serverless Redis, Vector, and QStash message queue from Upstash. Low-latency key-value store, vector similarity search, and HTTP-based message queuing.

env_required.description: Upstash Redis REST URL — from https://console.upstash.com

env_optional.description: Upstash Vector REST URL (for vector search)

servers.name: Resend
servers.description: Send transactional and marketing emails via the Resend API. Manage domains, audiences, contacts, and email templates.

env_required.description: Resend API key — get one at https://resend.com/api-keys

servers.name: Telegram
servers.description: Send and receive Telegram messages, manage chats, and interact with bots via the Telegram Bot API.

env_required.description: Telegram Bot token — create a bot via @BotFather at https://t.me/BotFather

env_optional.description: Default chat ID to send messages to

servers.name: Obsidian
servers.description: Read, write, and search notes in an Obsidian vault via the Local REST API plugin. Create, update, and link notes; query with Dataview; run templates.

env_required.description: Obsidian Local REST API key — install the Local REST API community plugin first
Obsidian REST API host e.g. https://127.0.0.1:27124

servers.name: Todoist
servers.description: Manage tasks, projects, labels, and filters in Todoist. Create, complete, and organise tasks; query by project or due date.

env_required.description: Todoist API token — get one at https://todoist.com/prefs/integrations

servers.name: Snowflake
servers.description: Run SQL queries, explore schemas, manage warehouses, and interact with Snowflake data cloud. Supports SELECT, DDL, and DML operations.

servers.name: Grafana
servers.description: Query dashboards, panels, and data sources from Grafana. Search and retrieve dashboard JSON, execute data source queries, and manage alerts.

env_required.description: Grafana service account token or API key

servers.name: PagerDuty
servers.description: Manage incidents, on-call schedules, and services in PagerDuty. Trigger, acknowledge, and resolve incidents; query on-call schedules and escalation policies.

env_required.description: PagerDuty REST API token — create one at Account Settings > API Access

env_optional.description: Default service ID for incident creation

servers.name: Zendesk
servers.description: Manage support tickets, users, and organisations in Zendesk. Create, update, and query tickets; search the help centre; manage macros and views.

env_required.description: Your Zendesk subdomain e.g. mycompany (from mycompany.zendesk.com)
Zendesk API token — generate one at Admin > Channels > API

servers.name: PostHog
servers.description: Query product analytics data from PostHog. Run HogQL queries, retrieve event trends, funnel analysis, user properties, and feature flag status.

env_required.description: PostHog personal API key — create one at Settings > Personal API Keys
PostHog host URL e.g. https://app.posthog.com or your self-hosted URL
PostHog project ID (found in Project Settings)

servers.name: Mixpanel
servers.description: Query Mixpanel product analytics. Run JQL queries, retrieve event data, segmentation, funnels, and user profile information.

env_required.description: Mixpanel project token — found in Project Settings

servers.name: Intercom
servers.description: Manage customer conversations, contacts, and companies in Intercom. Search conversations, send messages, update contact attributes, and retrieve help articles.

env_required.description: Intercom access token — create one at Settings > Integrations > Developer Hub

servers.name: Netlify
servers.description: Deploy sites, manage functions, environment variables, and DNS via the Netlify API. Trigger builds, list deploys, and manage team members.

env_required.description: Netlify personal access token — create one at User Settings > Applications

servers.name: Linear (Extended)
servers.description: Full Linear project management integration — issues, cycles, projects, teams, labels, and comments. Richer capability set than the base Linear server.

env_required.description: Linear API key — create one at Settings > API > Personal API keys

servers.name: Anthropic Claude
servers.description: Call Claude models directly from within workflows. Supports text generation, structured output, vision, and tool use — useful for LLM-as-a-tool patterns.

env_required.description: Anthropic API key — get one at https://console.anthropic.com/settings/keys

servers.name: OpenAI (Extended)
servers.description: Access OpenAI's full API — GPT-4o, DALL-E 3, Whisper, TTS, embeddings, fine-tuning, and the Assistants API. Richer than the base OpenAI server.

env_required.description: OpenAI API key — get one at https://platform.openai.com/api-keys

servers.name: Google BigQuery
servers.description: Run SQL queries against Google BigQuery data warehouse. List datasets and tables, execute queries, and retrieve job results.

env_required.description: Path to GCP service account JSON key file
GCP project ID that contains your BigQuery datasets

servers.name: Notion (Extended)
servers.description: Full Notion API integration — pages, databases, blocks, and search. Create rich content, query databases with filters, and manage workspace structure.

env_required.description: Notion internal integration token — create one at https://www.notion.so/my-integrations

servers.name: Box
servers.description: Manage files, folders, and users in Box cloud storage. Upload, download, search, share, and collaborate on content.

env_required.description: Box developer token or OAuth2 access token
